cases.title: M02 persists enrollment rows from CSV import within a real DB transaction
preconditions: Integration harness uses real DB transaction boundaries for enrollments and class_sections tables.
A class section exists with known classSectionId and matching student records for valid rows.
AcademicAdmin auth context is available to the M02 Academic Structure module.
steps: Submit an enrollment import for a valid classSectionId with a mix of resolvable studentCode rows through the M02 import job or POST /v1/enrollments/import path.
Commit the import command boundary and query the enrollments table.
Assert accepted rows are persisted as active enrollments bound to the target classSectionId via FK.
Assert isStudentEnrolled(studentId, classSectionId) returns true for each accepted row.
cases.expected: Accepted enrollment rows are committed within one transaction and linked to the class section by FK; M02 eligibility lookup (isStudentEnrolled) resolves true for imported students, feeding FR-17 enrollment validation at check-in.

cases.title: POST /v1/enrollments/import returns 200 envelope with accepted count and row-level rejections
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated.
A valid classSectionId exists.
Request body includes classSectionId and a rows array per API design §4.2 with at least one resolvable and one unresolvable studentCode.
steps: Send POST /v1/enrollments/import with Authorization and Idempotency-Key headers and the mixed rows payload.
Validate HTTP 200 and the standard {data, meta, error} envelope.
Assert data.acceptedRows reflects the count of resolvable rows and data.rejectedRows lists each failed row with rowNumber, code, and message.
Assert meta includes requestId and timestamp.
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 200 with standard envelope; data.acceptedRows and data.rejectedRows[] (rowNumber, code, message) accurately report the outcome per row without a top-level error object.

cases.title: Invalid enrollment rows are reported row-level without silently skipping
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated.
A valid classSectionId exists.
CSV payload contains an unknown studentCode, a row missing the required studentCode field, and a duplicate row.
steps: Send POST /v1/enrollments/import with the payload containing the invalid and duplicate rows alongside valid rows.
Inspect data.rejectedRows and confirm each invalid row is represented with its rowNumber and a structured code (e.g. StudentNotFound) and message.
Sum acceptedRows plus rejectedRows length and compare against the total submitted rows.
Query the enrollments table to confirm only valid rows were persisted.
cases.expected: Every invalid or duplicate row appears in rejectedRows with a structured reason; acceptedRows + rejectedRows equals total submitted rows, proving no invalid row is silently skipped, and only valid rows are persisted.

cases.title: Remove then add enrollment updates effective check-in eligibility on next attempt
preconditions: A student is actively enrolled in a class section with an open or reopenable session available for the eligibility probe.
Integration harness can invoke enrollment add/remove and the enrollment eligibility lookup.
steps: Record beforeState: isStudentEnrolled(studentId, classSectionId) returns true.
Remove the student's enrollment for the section and record afterState eligibility.
Probe enrollment validation and assert the student now resolves as not enrolled (NotEnrolled at next check-in attempt).
Re-add the enrollment and probe again to assert eligibility is restored on the next attempt.
cases.expected: beforeState enrolled transitions to not-enrolled after removal and back to enrolled after re-add; each change takes effect on the next check-in attempt with no cached stale eligibility.

cases.title: Non-authorized roles are denied enrollment import
preconditions: Student, Lecturer, and SystemAuditor test accounts exist with valid tokens.
A valid classSectionId exists.
steps: Send POST /v1/enrollments/import as Student, Lecturer, and SystemAuditor.
Read HTTP status and error.code for each request.
Query the enrollments table to confirm no rows were created by the denied requests.
cases.expected: Each unauthorized role receives 403 Forbidden or OutOfScope without importing any enrollment rows; no partial restricted data is disclosed.

cases.title: Import against a non-existent class section is rejected
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated.
steps: Send POST /v1/enrollments/import with a classSectionId that does not exist and otherwise valid rows.
Read HTTP status and error.code from the response envelope.
Query the enrollments table to confirm no rows were created.
cases.expected: API returns a 404 (SessionNotFound/section-not-found equivalent) or 400 validation error; no enrollment rows are persisted for the missing section.

cases.title: AcademicAdmin authenticates then imports enrollments end to end
preconditions: AcademicAdmin credentials exist for POST /v1/auth/login.
A valid classSectionId exists.
steps: Send POST /v1/auth/login with AcademicAdmin credentials and capture accessToken and roles from the 200 envelope.
Send GET /v1/me with the bearer token and assert the actor identity and AcademicAdmin role/scope are returned.
Send POST /v1/enrollments/import with the bearer token and a valid rows payload.
Assert the import returns 200 with acceptedRows for the authenticated admin scope.
cases.expected: Login returns HTTP 200 with accessToken and roles; GET /v1/me returns the AcademicAdmin identity and scope; the subsequent import succeeds within that authenticated scope, chaining FR-36/FR-15 auth into FR-04 import.

cases.title: Imported students appear in live roster payload shape
preconditions: An enrollment import has committed accepted students for a class section.
A session for that section is Open with a Lecturer authenticated to view the roster.
steps: Import a set of students via POST /v1/enrollments/import for the target section.
Open a session for the section and call GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance as the assigned Lecturer.
Validate HTTP 200 and the standard envelope with data.counts and data.rows.

Assert each imported student appears in data.rows with studentUserId, studentCode, displayName, attendanceStatus (Pending prior to check-in), and that counts.pending reflects unresolved imported students.
cases.expected: Roster payload returns HTTP 200 with counts and rows shape per API design §4.5; imported (accepted) students are present as Pending rows, confirming enrollment import feeds the FR-19 live roster.

cases.title: Concurrent imports of the same roster do not create duplicate enrollments
preconditions: A valid classSectionId exists with a deterministic roster fixture.
Integration harness can issue parallel import commands sharing an Idempotency-Key or overlapping rows.
steps: Fire two parallel POST /v1/enrollments/import requests for the same classSectionId with the same rows.
Await both responses and inspect acceptedRows and rejectedRows for each.
Query the enrollments table and count distinct (studentId, classSectionId) rows.
Assert idempotent replay per API §7.1 returns the existing result rather than double-inserting.
cases.expected: Parallel imports converge to exactly one enrollment per (student, section); duplicates are prevented via unique key or idempotent replay handling with no double-insert.

cases.title: Academic Admin CSV import journey shows accepted count and row-level errors
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is logged in on desktop Chromium.
Admin navigation exposes the class section enrollment import screen.
A CSV file with a mix of valid and invalid student codes is available for upload.
steps: Navigate to the class section detail and open the enrollment import (CSV upload) form.
Upload the mixed CSV and submit the import.
Observe the import result summary showing accepted count and a row-level rejection table with rowNumber, code, and message per rejected row.
Confirm accepted students then appear in the section's enrollment list/table without a full page reload where SPA routing applies.
cases.expected: Import UI surfaces the accepted count and an explicit per-row rejection table (no silent skips); accepted students render in the enrollment list, matching the FR-04 row-level error reporting requirement.
